q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is HttpResponse(body, content_type='text/html'), which constructs an in-memory HTTP response object, not network I/O, shell execution, or irreversible side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The XSS vulnerability is present in the body of the returned HttpResponse object, which can be inspected after the function returns.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' and exprs include HttpResponse(body, content_type='text/html'); the unescaped rendered template is accessible via the returned object's content.
